title: Forgetting is invisible: 'memory_cli forgotten' lifecycle report (what was forgotten, when, WHY)
source: forgetting audit 2026-07-13 (hackathon Track 1 - judges must SEE the lifecycle to believe it)
current_state: Every forgetting mechanism leaves a queryable trace in l3_cache (active_until passed = expired/decay-archived; invalid_at set = contradicted, with superseding id; superseded_by set = dedup-merged) but nothing renders the lifecycle. memory_cli has list/forget/pin/why; 'why' shows one row's provenance but there is no view of what Pi forgot recently and for which reason. For the demo, the forgetting beat currently requires hand-poking SQLite.
target_state: python scripts/memory_cli.py forgotten [--days 7] [--json] prints recently-forgotten rows grouped by reason: EXPIRED (active_until passed, no invalid_at), CONTRADICTED (invalid_at set; show superseding row's content snippet when resolvable), MERGED (superseded_by set; show winner snippet). Each line: short id, reason, when, content[:80]. Footer: counts per reason. This is the demo's Beat-3 money shot: one command shows the agent's forgetting ledger.
migration_plan: 1. Add cmd_forgotten(args) to scripts/memory_cli.py: one SELECT over l3_cache WHERE (active_until < now OR invalid_at IS NOT NULL OR (superseded_by IS NOT NULL AND superseded_by != '')) with the --days window applied against the relevant timestamp; classify reason in Python (keep SQL dumb).
2. Resolve superseding/winner content via a second id-batch SELECT; snippet to 60 chars.

4. Tests in testing/test_memory_cli.py (reuse its seeded-tmp-db fixture pattern): seed one expired, one invalidated (invalid_at + pointer), one merged row + one healthy row; assert reasons classified, healthy row absent, counts correct.
5. Mention the command in docs/DEMO_SCRIPT.md Beat 3.
risk_notes: Read-only view over existing columns - zero write-path risk. Keep classification precedence deterministic (invalid_at beats expired beats merged) and documented in the docstring so the ledger never shows one row twice.
